author: Linus Torvalds
quote: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.

author: Martin Fowler
quote: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.

author: Donald Knuth
quote: Premature optimization is the root of all evil.

author: Rob Pike
quote: Data dominates. If you've chosen the right data structures and organized things well, the algorithms will almost always be self-evident.

author: Edsger W. Dijkstra
quote: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.

author: Brian Kernighan
quote: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place. Therefore, if you write the code as cleverly as possible, you are, by definition, not smart enough to debug it.

author: Robert C. Martin
quote: The only way to go fast is to go well.

author: Jeff Atwood
quote: Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight.

author: Fred Brooks
quote: Adding manpower to a late software project makes it later.

author: Alan Kay
quote: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.

author: Tony Hoare
quote: There are two ways to write error-free programs; only the third one works.

author: John Johnson
quote: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.

author: Phil Karlton
quote: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.

author: Cory House
quote: Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it's bad.

author: Bill Gates
quote: Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight.

author: Bjarne Stroustrup
quote: There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.

author: Grace Hopper
quote: The most dangerous phrase in the language is, 'We've always done it this way.'

author: Edsger W. Dijkstra
quote: The question of whether a computer can think is no more interesting than the question of whether a submarine can swim.

author: David Heinemeier Hansson
quote: Make something people want, then make it run on the web.

author: Joel Spolsky
quote: It's harder to read code than to write it.

author: Ken Thompson
quote: When in doubt, use brute force.

author: Dennis Ritchie
quote: UNIX is basically a simple operating system, but you have to be a genius to understand the simplicity.

author: Steve Jobs
quote: Simple can be harder than complex: You have to work hard to get your thinking clean to make it simple.

author: Paul Graham
quote: A startup is a company designed to grow fast.

author: Guido van Rossum
quote: Code is read much more often than it is written.

author: Larry Wall
quote: The three chief virtues of a programmer are: Laziness, Impatience, and Hubris.

author: Eric Raymond
quote: Every good work of software starts by scratching a developer's personal itch.

author: Harlan Mills
quote: The bearing of a child takes nine months, no matter how many women are assigned.

author: Richard Feynman
quote: What I cannot create, I do not understand.

author: Ward Cunningham
quote: The best programmers are not marginally better than merely good ones. They are an order-of-magnitude better, measured by whatever standard one chooses.

author: Kent Beck
quote: I'm not a great programmer; I'm a good programmer with great habits.

author: Martin Fowler
quote: If you have to work with code that is a mess, the way to clean it up is to first write tests for it.

author: Alan Turing
quote: A computer would deserve to be called intelligent if it could deceive a human into believing that it was human.

author: Niklaus Wirth
quote: Controlling complexity is the essence of computer programming.

author: Leslie Lamport
quote: A distributed system is one in which the failure of a computer you didn't even know existed can render your own computer unusable.

author: Knuth
quote: An algorithm must be seen to be believed.

author: Jeff Dean
quote: The key to performance is elegance, not battalions of special cases.

author: Sandi Metz
quote: The purpose of abstraction is not to be vague, but to create a new semantic level in which one can be absolutely precise.

author: Andrew Hunt
quote: Don't repeat yourself. Every piece of knowledge must have a single, unambiguous, authoritative representation within a system.

author: Dave Thomas
quote: Good design adds value faster than it adds cost.

author: Eric Evans
quote: The heart of software is its ability to solve domain-related problems for its user.

author: Peter Norvig
quote: Lisp is worth learning for the profound enlightenment experience you will have when you finally get it.

author: Glenford Myers
quote: Testing shows the presence of bugs, not their absence.

author: Jeff Bezos
quote: If you're not stubborn, you'll give up on experiments too soon. And if you're not flexible, you'll pound your head against the wall and you won't see a different solution to a problem you're trying to solve.

author: James Gosling
quote: It's not that I'm so smart, it's just that I stay with problems longer.

author: Anonymous
quote: First make it work, then make it right, then make it fast.

author: Anonymous
quote: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.

author: Anonymous
quote: In software, the most beautiful code, the most beautiful functions, and the most beautiful programs are sometimes not there at all.

author: Anonymous
quote: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.

author: Linus Torvalds
quote: Most good programmers do programming not because they expect to get paid or get adulation by the public, but because it is fun to program.
